known_gaps: External guideline artifacts are not cached or license-verified.
Clinical and safety review is incomplete.
Simulation set is below release coverage requirements.

exposure.possible_poisoning.wording: 약물, 세제, 화학물질, 독성 식물 등 해로운 물질을 삼켰을 가능성이 있나요?

exposure.recent_international_travel.wording: 최근 해외여행이나 위생 환경이 다른 지역 방문이 있었나요?

exposure.sick_contact_gastrointestinal.wording: 가족, 직장, 학교 등 주변에 비슷한 구토나 설사를 한 사람이 있나요?

exposure.suspect_food_or_water.wording: 증상 전에 상했을 수 있는 음식, 덜 익힌 음식, 비살균 식품 또는 의심되는 물을 먹거나 마셨나요?

medication.recent_antibiotics.wording: 최근 3개월 안에 항생제를 복용했거나 입원한 적이 있나요?

patient.immunocompromised.wording: 면역을 낮추는 약을 사용하거나 면역이 약해지는 질환·치료를 받고 있나요?

pregnancy.possible.wording: 현재 임신 중이거나 임신 가능성이 있나요? 해당되지 않으면 그렇게 답해 주세요.

symptom.bloody_diarrhea.wording: 설사에 피가 섞였거나 항문에서 피가 나왔나요?

symptom.confusion.wording: 평소와 다르게 혼란스럽거나 심하게 처지거나 반응이 둔한가요?

symptom.dehydration_signs.wording: 입이 매우 마르거나 심하게 갈증이 나거나 일어설 때 어지럽나요?

symptom.diarrhea.count_24h.wording: 지난 24시간 동안 묽은 변을 몇 번 봤나요?

symptom.diarrhea.present.wording: 평소보다 묽거나 물 같은 변을 보고 있나요?

symptom.diarrhea.severity.wording: 설사는 가벼움, 반복됨, 매우 잦아 일상생활이 어려움 중 어디에 가깝나요?

symptom.dyspnea.wording: 숨쉬기가 힘든가요? 가벼움, 중간, 심함으로 답해도 됩니다.

symptom.hematemesis.wording: 피를 토했거나 구토물이 커피 찌꺼기처럼 보였나요?

symptom.persistent_abdominal_or_back_pai.wording: 계속되거나 반복되는 복통 또는 등 통증이 함께 있나요?

symptom.reduced_urine_output.wording: 소변 양이나 횟수가 평소보다 뚜렷하게 줄었거나 매우 진해졌나요?

symptom.sudden_severe_abdominal_pain.wording: 갑자기 시작한 참기 어려운 복통이 있나요?

symptom.unable_to_keep_fluids.wording: 물을 조금씩 마셔도 계속 토해서 전혀 유지할 수 없나요?

symptom.vomiting.severity.wording: 구토는 가벼움, 반복되지만 일부 섭취 가능, 매우 잦음 중 어디에 가깝나요?

symptom.vomiting_diarrhea.presentation.wording: 주된 증상은 구토, 설사, 둘 다 중 무엇인가요?

elements.limitation: Clinical sex used for interview routing is not automatically equivalent to every USCDI sex-related element.

elements.data_element: Medication, Dose, Dose Unit, Indication and Dispense Status
Blood Pressure, Heart Rate, Respiratory Rate, Temperature and Pulse Oximetry
Laboratory Test, Result, Result Status and Specimen
Clinical Test and Result or Report
Patient Goals and Care Experience Preference
Encounter Type, Time, Location and Disposition
Care Team Member Identity, Role and Contact
Medication, Laboratory, Imaging and Clinical Test Orders
History and Physical or Progress Note

limitations: Coverage indicates candidate data-population capability, not US certification or conformance.
A mapped Fact does not make the data element clinically required for this encounter.
US-specific vocabularies and regulatory semantics do not replace Korean bindings or policy.

elements.display: Patient concern, goal and expected outcome
Pain presence and NRS score when pain applies
Patient, proxy or record information source

guideline_behavior.rationale: Interview behavior derived from guidelines may change with recommendations.

local_jurisdiction.rationale: Local pathways and administrative rules may change quickly.

safety_critical.rationale: Safety behavior and escalation knowledge require frequent monitoring.

stable_semantic.rationale: Atomic patient-reportable concepts are relatively stable but still require periodic review.

terminology_mapping.rationale: Terminology and interoperability baselines have independent releases.

clinical_guideline.rationale: Daily automated monitoring provides early detection for high-impact guideline changes; full review occurs only when change is detected or the review date is reached.

interoperability_standard.rationale: Versioned interoperability standards and domain datasets are monitored weekly; detected changes trigger mapping and Coverage review without changing clinical rules.

nice_guidance.rationale: NICE guidance is versioned and may be amended; weekly monitoring detects changes without implying weekly clinical re-review.

public_health_guidance.rationale: Public-health pages can change between formal releases; weekly monitoring is appropriate for the research package.

terminology_server.rationale: Terminology servers publish versioned content; monthly baseline monitoring balances stability and acquisition cost.

assertions: Adults should seek urgent advice for inability to keep fluids down, bloody diarrhoea or rectal bleeding, persistent diarrhoea over seven days, vomiting over two days or ongoing dehydration.

Vomiting blood or coffee-ground material, green vomit in adults, suspected poisoning, sudden severe abdominal pain, severe breathing difficulty, confusion or abnormal responsiveness requires emergency assessment.
Older age, weakened immunity and other conditions can increase dehydration risk.
Most uncomplicated episodes are short-lived and infection control includes handwashing and staying away from work until 48 hours after the last diarrhoea or vomiting episode.
artifacts.title: Diarrhoea and vomiting

assertions: Reduced or dark urine, dry mouth, thirst, dizziness on standing and worsening alertness can indicate dehydration.
Vomiting and diarrhoea cause loss of fluid and electrolytes; oral rehydration may be appropriate when emergency features are absent.
artifacts.title: Dehydration

assertions: Severe foodborne illness features include bloody diarrhoea, diarrhoea over three days, high fever, frequent vomiting preventing fluid intake and dehydration.
Pregnancy, age 65 or older and weakened immunity increase risk of severe foodborne illness.
Food exposure, illness contacts and symptom timing inform assessment but do not establish an organism.
artifacts.title: Food Poisoning Symptoms

assertions: Persistent unexplained nausea, vomiting or diarrhoea with weight loss, upper abdominal pain, back pain or age-dependent features can require investigation consideration.
The interview records persistence and weight loss without assigning cancer.
artifacts.title: Suspected cancer: recognition and referral — nausea, vomiting and diarrhoea

assertions: STOM confirmed active 422400008 Vomiting (disorder) and 62315008 Diarrhea (finding).
Finding site and Severity were allowed for both focus concepts.
MRCM validates terminology modeling only and does not determine clinical questions, priority, diagnosis or safety.
artifacts.title: STOM SNOMED CT lookup and MRCM allowed attributes for vomiting and diarrhea
